items.title: Federal court blocks USDA-approved SNAP food-restriction pilots in five states
items.summary: On June 22, 2026, a federal district court held USDA exceeded its authority when it approved state SNAP demonstration projects restricting what participants can buy with benefits. The order stops the pilots in Colorado, Iowa, Nebraska, Tennessee, and West Virginia, while other states’ restrictions may continue unless separately withdrawn or struck down.

items.title: CMS interim final rule tightens ‘medical frailty’ exemptions in Medicaid work requirements
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule (June 1, 2026) that adopts a more restrictive definition of medical frailty for Medicaid work requirements and bars states from adding extra categorical exemptions. KFF notes work requirements must begin January 1, 2027 (or sooner at state option) in the 43 states covered by the 2025 reconciliation law, and states may use self-declaration in limited cases through 2027.

items.title: Social Security confirms 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI increase begins Dec. 31, 2025
items.summary: SSA says Social Security and SSI benefits will rise 2.8% for 2026. The increase applies to Social Security benefits payable in January 2026, and SSI recipients see the higher payment starting December 31, 2025.

items.title: USDA sets FY2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit amounts
items.summary: USDA’s WIC policy memo lists the FY2026 monthly cash-value benefit at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA notes FY2026 runs from Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.

items.title: New WIC income guidelines take effect July 1 (2026–2027)
items.summary: USDA published the 2026–2027 WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the annual federal poverty guideline example is $33,000 for a household of 4 (and $15,960 for a household of 1).

items.title: HUD sets 2026 deadlines for EIV-SAVE review/attestation for housing providers
items.summary: HUD set deadlines for housing providers to review their EIV-SAVE report and attest documentation is error-free for people who claim U.S. citizenship or eligible noncitizen status. NAHRO reports the PBRA owner/agent deadline is June 20, 2026, and Housing Choice Voucher/Public Housing agencies must complete the attestation by August 3, 2026.

items.title: ED updates 2026–27 FAFSA processing specifications (June 2026 update)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted a June 2026 update to the 2026–27 FAFSA Specifications Guide for schools and vendors (last modified June 22, 2026). These technical specs affect how institutions and software providers process FAFSA data for the 2026–27 aid year.
items.source: U.S. Department of Education (Federal Student Aid)
